text.title: Engagement Pattern Memory — Output Contract
text.summary: Pins the engagement memory file shape + session-update record. Contractor (or supporting agent) must satisfy these contracts.

forbidden.pattern: memory file shared across multiple engagements (path / filename does not include engagement_id) (r1)
glossary entries that lack scope_marker or that explicitly state cross-engagement applicability (r3)
session_updates field empty for a session where work was done (r2 — write discipline skipped)
entries older than 90 days without a "still current" annotation OR archive flag (r4)
reviewer_preferences entries that name a reviewer but lack any concrete preference bullets
recurring_traps without a date + fix-or-workaround status
memory file loaded into the wrong engagement's context (mixed-context session) (r5)

allowed-transformations.transformation: Cluster near-duplicate convention entries (e.g., two mentions of the same lint rule).
Move retired-pattern entries to an "archive" section instead of deleting (audit trail).
Reference an engagement-shared parent if the engagement is part of a multi-project client portfolio (rare; documented at top of file).

list.item: Session updates list at least one observation OR an explicit "no new patterns" entry (r2)
No glossary or convention text references "all my clients" / "across projects" (r1, r3)
Memory file size <= 4000 tokens (load-efficient); if larger, run scripts/decay-cleanup.py (r4)
Hook script loads ONLY the active engagement's memory (r5)
Agent confirms engagement identity at start of session ("loaded engagement X memory; active since DATE")
If contractor switches engagement mid-day, session restarts with new memory loaded
